word: G1613 - ἐκταράσσω - ektarássō - ek-tar-as'-so - Exceedingly trouble

sections.paragraph:
- Original: ἐκταράσσω

- Transliteration: Ektarasso

- Phonetic: ek-tar-as'-so

- Definition:

1. to agitate, trouble, exceedingly

- Origin: from G1537 and G5015

- TDNT entry: None

- Part(s) of speech: Verb

- Strong's: From G1537 and G5015; to disturb wholly: - exceedingly trouble.
